details: Paper organic_synthesis/fernandes_2018_pyridazine_suzuki.pdf line 1185 contains verbatim 'Yellow solid (14%). Mp: 207-210 ◦ C.' for compound 3b. DOI, compound name, value, data_type all match.

Quote 'Table 1: fenclofenac Melting point (deg C) 112' is a constructed paraphrase. The actual paper (2009_Yalkowsky_An_interesting_relationship_between_drug.pdf line 419) row reads 'Fenclofenac 297 112 g 1.00 c 400 c −4.55 e 4.71' — the constructed quote string is not present verbatim in the paper. Value 112 °C is correct.

Quote 'Table I. 1,2-diiodobenzene MP 27 BP 286' is a constructed paraphrase not present verbatim. The 1990 Yalkowsky paper (OCR) row reads '1,2-Diiodobenzene 27 286' with column headers elsewhere. Value 27 is correct.

Quote 'Table 1: nicardipine Melting point (deg C) 137' is a constructed paraphrase. Paper line 546 reads 'Nicardipine 466 137 g 1.00 b 25 b(avg) −5.33 e 5.51' — header concatenation absent. Value 137 is correct.

Paper 176_PMC11206253 article.pdf (laid-out, around line 2517-2522) contains verbatim 'Recrystallization of the product from hot ethanol afforded shiny yellow microcrystals (35.4% yield) with mp 146–147 ◦ C' for compound 8a. DOI/PMC, name, value, data_type all match.
Quote 'Table 1: nisoldipine Melting point (deg C) 152' is a constructed paraphrase. Paper line 567 reads 'Nisoldipine 388 152 j 0.95 b, c(avg) 15 b, c(avg) −5.63 h 4.86'. Value 152 is correct.

Quote 'm.p. 260-262 (Table 1, N-benzoyl-2-(4-nitrophenyl)-2-cyano-6,7-dihydro-9,...)' is a fabricated description, not verbatim text. Paper 054_PMC6146489 Table 1 row reads '13c brown 79 260-262 C29H22N4O5 68.77 4.35 11.07 -'. Value 260-262 matches; compound full name appears elsewhere as 'N-benzoylimino derivatives 13b,c' (line 262).

Compound name in row 'Methyl 4-((E)-benzylidene-1-phenyl-2',5'-dioxo) spiro compound' does not match paper's name '(E)-Methyl 4-((2',5'-dioxo-1'-phenylspiro[isochroman-3,3'-pyrrolidin]-4'-ylidene)methyl)benzoate' (organic_synthesis/yanovich_2024_spiroheterocycles_rh_catalysis.pdf line 803). Quote 'mp 189.2–189.9 °C' is verbatim at line 805 and value matches, but compound name is materially wrong (missing isochroman-pyrrolidin core).

Quote 'Table 2: 1,4-dioxane exp. mp 11 deg C' is a paraphrase. Paper (the file named 2009_Dearden_... is actually 2003 paper, DOI 10.1897/01-363) Table 2 line 359 reads '1,4-Dioxane 101.0 102.8 98.4 102.9 60.0 99.8 102.8 3.707 ... 11.8 263.6 234.5 274.0'. Value ~12 (rounded to 11) is approximately correct (exp mp column shows 11.8) but quote not verbatim.

Paper 014_PMC12943640 article.pdf lines 571-573 contains the quote substantively verbatim (with NFC/whitespace normalization): 'C5 H13 NO6 P2 : 244.0140, found 244.0138. N-(Piperidylamino)methylenebisphosphonic acid (27) was obtained with a 61% yield; mp. 241–244 ◦ C; 31 P NMR (242.93 MHz, D2 O + NaOD)'. Compound, value 241-244, data_type all match. DOI ph19020256 matches paper (note: row source field says vol 18 2025 but paper is vol 19 2026 — source URL/DOI agree with paper).

Paper organic_synthesis/yanovich_2024_spiroheterocycles_rh_catalysis.pdf line 579 names compound 2f as '(E)-7-Benzyl-9-benzylidene-4-methyl-1-oxa-7-azaspiro[4.4]non-3-ene-2,6,8-trione', and line 582 gives 'mp 167.2-168.0 °C' (using a Unicode dash). Compound name matches, value matches, data_type measured matches.

Quote 'II 340.34 C 17 H 16 N 4 O 4 60.00 (60.11) 4.74 (4.62) 16.46 (16.24) 112–114 Yellowish white' does not appear in paper 073_PMC13085778 article.pdf — no Table 1 with this content is present in the extracted text (only references to Table 1 for elemental analysis exist). The original row notes already flag this. No verifiable mp 112-114 for compound II in the available paper text.

Paper materials_inorganic/rogers_2021_il_ethoxylation_lowering.pdf line 947-949 reads '[Ca][C8E8c]2 is already liq- uid above 19.5 °C'. Compound name [Ca][C8E8c]2 matches, value 19.5 °C matches, relation '~' (approximate) appropriate for liquid-solid transition statement.

Quote 'Table 8: phenol MP 41 deg C' is a paraphrase. Paper 2017_Yalkowsky... Table 8 (line 2645+) has 'mono- 41' under the 'Hydroxy Benzenes / OH' column; the compound 'phenol' is not literally written in Table 8 alongside '41'. Value 41 °C is correct for mono-hydroxybenzene = phenol but quote not verbatim.

Quote 'Table 6: pyrrolidine MP -61 deg C' is a paraphrase. Paper 2017_Yalkowsky line 2570 reads 'diethyl amine -50 NIST* pyrrolidine -61 NIST* -11'. Value -61 °C is correct but the constructed quote is not present verbatim.

Paper 072_PMC12943595 article.pdf line 874 contains compound name '4.2.4. N-(3-Methoxybenzyl)-6-chloro-4-hydroxy-2-quinolone-3-carboxamide (9)' and line 882 reads 'm.p: 230–233 ◦ C'. Compound, value, data_type match. Note: row's stated evidence_location says 'Section 4.2.3' but the compound (9) is actually in Section 4.2.4 in the paper — minor location label off, but quote and substance verified.

Paper measurement_prediction/muller_2020_dsc-tga-pcm-thermophysical.pdf line 643 (Table 5) reads 'Methanoic acid 64-18-6 Fatty Acid 233.81 ± 0.95 −50.4 ± 0.02 −49.4 ± 0.01 −38.74–−33.74 n/a 1'. Quote matches verbatim with whitespace. Melting Peak column gives -49.4 °C — data_type DSC_peak/measured matches.

Quote 'Table 1: hexamethylethane mp/K 373.9' is a paraphrase. Paper 2000_Brown line 62 reads 'C8H18 octane 216.4 3-methylheptane 152.7 hexamethylethane 373.9'. Value 373.9 K and conversion arithmetic to 100.75 °C are correct, but the constructed quote is not present verbatim.

Paper 114_PMC13093879 article_text.txt contains verbatim (at offset ~55103): '...chromene-3-carboxamide (12r) Brown solid; yield: 73%; MP = 247–249 °C'. Compound name, value 247-249, data_type all match.

Compound name in row 'Chloride (2) To a solution of 5-azaindole' is garbled/truncated. The paper 036_PMC6193216 article.pdf line ~534 names the compound '5-(Сyanomethyl)-1H-pyrrolo[3,2-c]pyridin-5-ium Chloride (2)'. Quote '31 g (80%); mp 212-214 °С.' is truncated from '1.31 g (80%); mp 212–214 °С.' Value 212-214 correct, but compound name is unusable.

Compound name 'H )-yl)thiazolidin-4-one (V2)' is a malformed fragment. Paper 033_PMC7148931 line 178-180 has '5-(3-Chlorobenzylidene)-2-(2-chloroquinolin-3-yl)-3-(4-oxo-2-phenylquinazolin-3(4H)-yl)thiazolidin-4-one (V)2. Yield, 66%, light brown crystalline solid, mp 148–150 C.' Quote substance matches and value 148-150 correct, but compound_name field is corrupted/unusable.

Paper 072_PMC12943595 article.pdf line 1179 contains '4.2.19. N-(3-Bromobenzyl)-6-chloro-4-hydroxy-2-quinolone-3-carboxamide (24)' and line 1194 reads 'm.p: 292–294 ◦ C'. Compound, value, data_type match. Row's evidence_location says 4.2.18 but the compound is in 4.2.19 in the paper — minor location label off by one section.

Paper measurement_prediction/muller_2020_dsc-tga-pcm-thermophysical.pdf line 645 (Table 5) reads 'Propionic acid 79-09-4 Fatty Acid 143.7 ± 1.44 −25.7 ± 0.15 −22.2 ± 0.08 −46.6–−44.49 n/a 1'. Quote matches verbatim. Melting Onset column = -25.7 °C — data_type DSC_onset/measured matches.

Quote 'Table 2 Preparative details, melting points (uncorrected) and IR solid spectra of the purified iodylarenes obtained. R=4-F Mp ( o C) 262 expl.' is a fabricated concatenation of caption text and a row cell, not a verbatim string. Paper 139_PMC6236348 line 350 reads '4-F 12 91 262 expl. 248 [28] 766, 742 (?), 713 (IO2)'. Value 262 (decomposition) is correct but constructed quote not verbatim.

Paper 079_PMC10672842 article.pdf line 1114 names compound 'Synthesis of 1-(2,4-Difluorophenyl)-2-(2-nitro-1H-imidazol-1-yl)ethanol (9)' and line 1122 contains verbatim 'melting point: 184–186 ◦ C'. Compound, value, data_type all match.
